name: HTSP Tuner
description: Live TV and DVR for Jellyfin backed by Tvheadend over its native HTSP protocol. Unlike the official Tvheadend plugin -- which speaks HTSP only for metadata and then pulls the actual video over plain HTTP -- this plugin subscribes to the HTSP stream directly and remuxes the elementary streams into MPEG-TS inside Jellyfin. Codec, resolution, language and channel layout come straight from the HTSP subscriptionStart event; a short, one-time probe of the plugin's own muxed output then fills in the fields HTSP cannot carry -- interlacing, HDR/colour, bit depth, profile and level -- so Jellyfin makes correct direct-play, deinterlace and transcode decisions. All of this happens without opening a second tuner subscription. Add it under Live TV -> Add Tuner Device; a matching HTSP guide is registered automatically. Supports multiple Tvheadend servers, fast same-multiplex channel switching, radio channels, live EPG pushed over async metadata, recording via Jellyfin's DVR, channel tags, stream sharing between clients, and tuner/signal statistics.
overview: Native HTSP streaming from Tvheadend, remuxed locally to MPEG-TS.

versions.changelog:
- Fix playback dying when you turn on subtitles. DVB subtitles were reported under
  ffmpeg's codec name, so Jellyfin mistook a picture subtitle for a text one and tried
  to convert it to text before playing it. They are now burned in correctly.
- Stop leaving Tvheadend subscriptions running forever. Jellyfin does not always close
  a stream it opened -- a channel could keep streaming and holding a tuner all night
  after you stopped watching. A stream nobody is reading now closes itself after 60s.
- Stop piling up HTSP connections. Every Jellyfin restart stranded a connection that
  stayed open, kept reconnecting, and kept re-downloading the whole guide; they are now
  closed on shutdown.
- Label the auto-registered HTSP guide so it is identifiable in the guide provider list.

- Show where a channel is tuned from -- network, satellite position and mux (e.g.
  "Hispasat (30W) . 12476H") -- in Jellyfin's playback media info.
- Fetch channel icons from Tvheadend over HTSP (fileOpen/fileRead) and serve them from the plugin, so it no longer needs any HTTP access to Tvheadend. Removed the HTTP port/HTTPS/web-root settings.
- Fix live streams freezing on one frame: reuse a single HTSP connection per server (which reconnects itself) instead of building a new connection + full re-sync on every momentary disconnect.

- Stable channel ids keyed by the Tvheadend server instead of the per-add tuner GUID, so removing and re-adding a tuner no longer multiplies channels. (One-time id change: re-add the tuner once to drop old duplicates.)
- Honour Tvheadend's subscriptionGrace when starting a channel, so slow-tuning muxes (e.g. satellite LNB lock) are no longer cut off by a fixed 15s timeout.
- Fix HEVC channels black-screening on direct-play clients: set the MPEG-TS random_access_indicator on key frames (and data_alignment on video PES) so a player can find where to start decoding.
- Tuner-only model: the plugin does nothing until you add an HTSP tuner device. Removed the config-only integrated service and the unused Streaming mode setting.
- Fix the plugin settings page: fields now load and persist (it referenced a controller that did not exist, so it never initialised).
- Initial release: native HTSP Live TV & DVR for Jellyfin -- native subscription streaming with in-process MPEG-TS remux, EPG, subtitles, multi-audio, radio, channel tags.
